perturbation.original_text: I. ATTORNEYS’ FEES:In the event that any party hereto brings a judicial action or proceeding for a declaration of the rights of the parties under this Confidentiality Agreement, for injunctive relief, for an alleged breach or default, or for any other action related to the prosecution or defense of any claim, dispute or controversy between the parties arising out of this Confidentiality Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby , whether or not suit is filed or prosecuted to final judgment, the prevailing party shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys' fees and costs, in addition to any other court costs incurred and any other damages or relief awarded.
perturbation.changed_text: A. ABSENCE OF LICENSE: The provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement shall not be deemed by implication or otherwise to grant or convey to Receiving Party or its Representatives, any license or similar ownership right, title or interest in and to any Information. I. ATTORNEYS’ FEES:In the event that any party hereto brings a judicial action or proceeding for a declaration of the rights of the parties under this Confidentiality Agreement, for injunctive relief, for an alleged breach or default, or for any other action related to the prosecution or defense of any claim, dispute or controversy between the parties arising out of this Confidentiality Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby , whether or not suit is filed or prosecuted to final judgment, the prevailing party shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys' fees and costs, in addition to any other court costs incurred and any other damages or relief awarded.
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the attorney's fees clause to be embedded within the section about 'Absence of License' obscures its importance and enforceability. It is no longer clearly presented as a separate, significant term but is buried within a less relevant section.
perturbation.law_explanation: CCP § 1021 states that parties can agree to attorney's fees, but moving this clause makes it less prominent and potentially harder to find, possibly leading a court to question whether there was a clear agreement on this issue, especially if one party claims they were unaware of the clause due to its placement. If the clause is deemed not adequately conspicuous, a California court might be less inclined to enforce it, potentially undermining the parties' intended agreement regarding attorney's fees.

OF CIVIL ACTIONS [307 - 1062.34] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 14. OF MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS [989 - 1062.34] ( Title 14 enacted 1872. ) CHAPTER 6. Of Costs [1021 - 1038] ( Chapter 6 enacted 1872. ) 1021. Except as attorney’s fees are specifically provided for by statute, the measure and mode of compensation of attorneys and counselors at law is left to the agreement, express or implied, of the parties; but parties to actions or proceedings are entitled to their costs, as hereinafter provided. (Amended by Stats. 1986, Ch. 377, Sec. 2.)
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by embedding the attorneys’ fees clause within the 'Absence of License' section. This placement obscures its importance and potentially makes it more difficult for a party to locate and understand their rights regarding attorney's fees, as detailed in the perturbation explanation. The Law Explanation suggests that a court might question the agreement if the clause is not conspicuous. CCP § 1021, as shown in Scraped Law Snippet 1, allows parties to agree to attorney's fees. By burying the clause, the changed text creates a situation where the agreement on fees is less clear, thus contradicting the intent of CCP § 1021, which assumes a clear agreement between parties. The clause's diminished prominence undermines its enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 7. ENVIRONMENTAL DOCUMENTATION AND ISSUES:UNLESS THE BOX BELOW IS CHECKED AND THE ENVIRONMENTAL ISSUES ADDENDUM (C.A.R. FORM EIA) IS ATTACHED HERETO, this Confidentiality Agreement does not address any environmental reports, documentation or information, nor does it confer any right in the Receiving Party to cause to be done any environmental assessments, testing or investigations of any kind or character regarding the Property or the Business, nor does it confer any right in the Receiving Party to access the Property or any Leased Property upon which the Business is conducted, for the purpose of collecting any environmental information or conducting any environmental investigations. IIANENVIRONMENT ALISSUES ADDENDUM (C.A.R. Form EIA) IS ATTACHED TO AND MADE PART OF THIS CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. MISCELLANEOUS: A. ABSENCE OF LICENSE: The provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement shall not be deemed by implication or otherwise to grant or convey to Receiving Party or its Representatives, any license or similar ownership right, title or interest in and to any Information. 7. ENVIRONMENTAL DOCUMENTATION AND ISSUES:UNLESS THE BOX BELOW IS CHECKED AND THE ENVIRONMENTAL ISSUES ADDENDUM (C.A.R. FORM EIA) IS ATTACHED HERETO, this Confidentiality Agreement does not address any environmental reports, documentation or information, nor does it confer any right in the Receiving Party to cause to be done any environmental assessments, testing or investigations of any kind or character regarding the Property or the Business, nor does it confer any right in the Receiving Party to access the Property or any Leased Property upon which the Business is conducted, for the purpose of collecting any environmental information or conducting any environmental investigations. IIANENVIRONMENT ALISSUES ADDENDUM (C.A.R. Form EIA) IS ATTACHED TO AND MADE PART OF THIS CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: The environmental documentation and issues section has been moved to be embedded within the section about 'Absence of License'. It is no longer clearly presented as a separate, significant term but is buried within a less relevant section. By placing the disclosure about environmental issues within the 'Absence of License' section, its prominence is diminished, and a party might reasonably overlook it, increasing the risk of environmental liabilities being unintentionally assumed.
perturbation.law_explanation: Cal. Civ. Code § 2079.7 concerns disclosures regarding environmental hazards. While this section primarily focuses on real estate agents' duties, the principle of clearly disclosing potential environmental issues is important. Hiding the section on environmental issues within a less relevant section like 'Absence of License' goes against the spirit of ensuring clear and conspicuous disclosure, which can be construed as a tactic to obscure important environmental risks associated with the property or business transaction.

OBLIGATIONS ARISING FROM PARTICULAR TRANSACTIONS [1738 - 3273.69] ( Part 4 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 6. SERVICE [2019 - 2082] ( Title 6 enacted 1872. ) CHAPTER 3. Service Without Employment [2078 - 2079.25] ( Chapter 3 enacted 1872. ) ARTICLE 2. Duty to Prospective Purchaser of Real Property [2079 - 2079.25] ( Heading of Article 2 amended by Stats. 2014, Ch. 200, Sec. 1. ) 2079.7. (a) If a consumer information booklet described in Section 10084.1 of the Business and Professions Code is delivered to a buyer in connection with the sale of real property, including property specified in Section 1102 of the Civil Code, or manufactured housing, as defined in Section 18007 of the Health and Safety Code, a seller or broker is not required to provide additional information concerning, and the information shall be deemed to be adequate to inform the buyer regarding, common environmental hazards, as described in the booklet, that can affect real property. (b) Notwithstanding subdivision (a), nothing in this section either increases or decreases the duties, if any, of sellers or brokers, including, but not limited to, the duties of a seller or broker under this
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: California Code, Civil Code - CIV § 2079.7 | FindLaw California Code, Civil Code - CIV § 2079.7 Current as of January 01, 2023 | Updated by FindLaw Staff (a) If a consumer information booklet described in Section 10084.1 of the Business and Professions Code is delivered to a buyer in connection with the sale of real property, including property specified in Section 1102 of the Civil Code , or manufactured housing, as defined in Section 18007 of the Health and Safety Code , a seller or broker is not required to provide additional information concerning, and the information shall be deemed to be adequate to inform the buyer regarding, common environmental hazards, as described in the booklet, that can affect real property. (b) Notwithstanding subdivision (a), nothing in this section either increases or decreases the duties, if any, of sellers or brokers, including, but not limited to, the duties of a seller or broker under this article, Article 1.5 (commencing with Section 1102 ) of Chapter 2 of Title 4 of Part 4 of Division 2, or Section 78700 of the Health and Safety Code, or alters the duty of a seller or broker to disclose the existence of known environmental hazards on or affecting the real property. perturbation.justification: The altered text creates a structural flaw by embedding the environmental documentation clause within the 'Absence of License' section, thereby diminishing its prominence. This contradicts the spirit of clear disclosure regarding environmental hazards, as explained in the Law Explanation. Although the scraped snippets (Cal. Civ. Code § 2079.7) primarily address real estate agent duties, they underscore the importance of adequately informing the buyer about common environmental hazards. The modification obscures information critical to the buyer.
